channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The latest: Federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers all over the country have responded with anger and confusion Friday toward President Donald Trump’s aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Red state AG promises legal fight with ICE-resisting local governments
item.description: Indiana AG Todd Rokita threatened Indianapolis schools and the city police department for refusing to cooperate with federal ICE agents to deport illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: U.S. conservatives have looked to Hungary's Viktor Orbán as an inspiration for a right-wing America, and the prime minister has become a close ally of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: 7 most likely GOP successors to Trump in 2028
item.description: President Trump raised eyebrows in an interview that aired Monday after he declined to name Vice President Vance as his automatic successor ahead of the 2028 presidential election. “No, but he is very capable,” Trump told fox News’ Bret Baier when asked if he viewed Vance as his 2028 successor.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's mugshot spotted hanging in ornate frame near Oval Office
item.description: Social media posts showed a framed front page 2023 mugshot of President Trump from the New York Post near the Oval Office in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New poll reveals growing number of Dems want party to move in new direction
item.description: After the Democratic Party's setbacks to Trump and Republicans in the 2024 elections, a new national poll indicates Democrats increasingly want their party to move toward the center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: Since President Donald Trump took office, immigrants in the U.S. illegally have changed their travel patterns and try to stay home, out of fear they could be deported.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sarah Palin ‘would love to’ join Trump administration
item.description: She pointed to "gatekeepers" within the administration, telling NewsNation: "It gets frustrating for those with a heart to serve.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Around 14 percent of Minnesotans have filed their 2024 taxes so far
item.description: Two months out from the tax filing deadline, the Minnesota Department of Revenue is already beginning to see a stream of return filings. The state is encouraging people to file electronically and pick the direct deposit option if they’re looking to get their returns quickly.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Calls for Mayor Adams’ resignation mount amid turmoil at DOJ
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Calls for Mayor Eric Adams’ resignation are mounting after the Department of Justice ordered his case to be dropped. One call came from Lt. Gov. Antonio Delgado, second-in-command to Gov. Kathy Hochul. Hochul is the only person with the power to remove Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Senate panel advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick to lead the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate Judiciary Committee voted along party lines on Thursday to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, pushing past Democratic concerns that he would operate as a loyalist for the president and target perceived adversaries of the White Hou
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A look at who might run for Sen. Tina Smith’s seat in the Senate
item.description: With Sen. Tina Smith’s announcement that she will not seek reelection in 2026, politicians on both sides of the aisle are eying her seat. Here are some of those who say they plan to run — or are at least considering it.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Skyrocketing fees: St. Pete condo owners say they never asked for bailout, only relief
item.description: Condo owners are waiting for answers on whether lawmakers will do anything to ease concerns over rising fees.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
